mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% show a positive return frequency below 55% or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stabilizes between 9.0% and 10.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive momentum signal yields a negative 60-day return frequency above 50%, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.01 over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of occurrences during early-cycle expansion phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with a statistically significant t-test (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month nonfarm payroll revisions reflect underlying economic resilience that supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a positive return frequency below 50% across all tested regimes, or rolling Rank IC remains persistently negative over 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent and rising retail sales confirm synchronized demand expansion that lifts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over univariate baselines in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in housing starts during a credit-constrained regime signals deteriorating construction sector fundamentals that drag on broader equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a positive return frequency exceeding 60%, or the signal loses significance when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.
